text:
Once upon a sunlit summer day, there was a cozy little bookstore nestled in the heart of the town. Inside this inviting store, the owner, Ms. Amelia, would often read aloud to her little mouse friend, Mr. Fluffington. Together, they discovered old books that had stories as rich as their own lives.

One particular afternoon, as they nibbled on some stale crumbs, Ms. Amelia told Mr. Fluffington about a brave little hedgehog named Harold. Harold lived in the Whispering Woods. Everyone said he could talk — just listen carefully during hibiscus seasons.

At a festival celebrating flowers, Harold decided he wanted to give something back to the woodland creatures. He concocted this marvelous invention — an enchanted talking bird feather. If you listened real hard during the night, you could hear him chuckle heartily.

Harold used up all his feather feathers telling tales, and soon he went on journeys. He explored caverns beneath the moonlit forest floor
